provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.monitoring: Contraindicated in significant renal impairment (approximately eGFR <25 mL/min); systemic exposure to absorbed drug/metabolites increases
Renally cleared and accumulates in impairment; reduce and closely monitor for neurotoxicity/encephalopathy. Avoid or use with extreme caution in severe impairment (eGFR <30) and in dialysis patients
Monitor renal function; caution in renal impairment owing to 5-ASA nephrotoxicity risk; avoid in significant/severe renal impairment
In renal impairment, sodium and bicarbonate handling are impaired - monitor for sodium/fluid overload, metabolic alkalosis and electrolytes; use with caution and reduced load
Reduce dose and titrate slowly as renal function declines due to oxypurinol accumulation; monitor for hypersensitivity
Dose reduction with moderate impairment and further reduction with severe impairment/dialysis as it is renally cleared
Renally cleared and neurotoxic in accumulation; reduce dose and extend interval as eGFR falls, and monitor for CNS/anticholinergic toxicity. Severe impairment approaches contraindication.
High hyperkalaemia risk in renal impairment; avoid when eGFR <30 and monitor potassium and renal function closely.
Closer monitoring of heart rate and blood pressure in severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance below ~20 mL/min); use with caution given ~50% renal elimination
Not recommended in severe renal impairment due to risk of metabolic acidosis and lack of clinical data
Monitor renal function and serum potassium, particularly in severe renal impairment
Predominantly renally cleared and accumulates in renal impairment; dose reduction required as eGFR falls (further reduction <15 mL/min). Monitor heart rate and for excess beta-blockade.
Predominantly renally excreted; in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30) use only if the expected benefit outweighs the risk and monitor for antimuscarinic effects.
Consider a lower starting dose in renal impairment; monitor serum potassium and renal function, particularly in advanced CKD, volume depletion or with concomitant NSAIDs/diuretics
Progressively less effective as eGFR falls; largely ineffective and generally avoided below approximately 30 mL/min/1.73m2. Contraindicated in anuria. Monitor electrolytes and renal function
Intrinsically nephrotoxic; titrate to blood level, monitor serum creatinine and eGFR regularly, and reduce dose for a sustained rise in creatinine

Assess renal function before initiation and periodically; glucose-lowering efficacy is reduced at lower eGFR and initiation for glycaemic control is not recommended below eGFR 45, though cardio-renal indications extend lower
Assess renal function before and periodically during therapy; progressive dose reduction with declining eGFR as the drug is predominantly renally cleared.
Contraindicated in moderate-to-severe renal impairment (CrCl below ~50 mL/min); monitor serum sodium
Dose adjustment required in severe renal impairment / end-stage renal disease; avoid dose escalation

Clonidine is substantially renally cleared and its half-life is prolonged in renal impairment; clonidine and its metabolites accumulate, so monitor blood pressure and for excess sedation and adjust according to response.

Avoid analgesic/anti-inflammatory doses in significant renal impairment (nephrotoxicity, sodium/fluid retention); low-dose antiplatelet aspirin may be continued with caution and renal-function monitoring. Avoid in severe impairment/end-stage disease.
Not recommended in severe renal impairment (CrCl <30 mL/min) due to unstudied risk of metabolic acidosis
Not recommended in severe renal impairment (CrCl <30 mL/min) due to accumulation of metabolites

Assess renal function before and periodically during treatment; glycaemic efficacy diminishes at lower eGFR and initiation for glucose lowering is not recommended at low eGFR, though cardio-renal indications extend to lower thresholds
Monitor serum creatinine/eGFR and potassium; enalaprilat accumulates with declining renal function
Monitor eGFR and potassium; caution and dose review in significant renal impairment
Reduce dose or extend dosing interval in moderate-severe renal impairment; watch for CNS effects (confusion, delirium) especially in the elderly
Limited data in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30); use with caution and monitor response

Assess renal function before and during therapy; active moiety (fenofibric acid) accumulates in renal impairment. Reduced exposure required in moderate impairment; avoid in severe impairment (approximately eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m2).
Avoid in severe renal impairment due to increased myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk; use caution and monitor for muscle symptoms in moderate impairment
Weakly active metabolites accumulate causing prolonged hypoglycaemia; avoid in significant renal impairment and monitor blood glucose closely if used
Thiazide-like diuretics lose antihypertensive efficacy in severe renal impairment and may worsen renal function — avoid; monitor electrolytes and creatinine

Renal impairment markedly increases risk of severe, prolonged hypoglycaemia (reduced clearance of drug and active M1 metabolite); use lowest effective dose and monitor blood glucose closely. Avoid in severe impairment.

Preferred over renally-cleared sulfonylureas as metabolites are inactive, but reduced clearance still raises hypoglycaemia risk in significant impairment; use cautiously with close glucose monitoring.

Maximum exposure should be limited in severe renal impairment (CrCl <=30 mL/min) and end-stage renal disease; supplementation is required after haemodialysis. Slower titration advised with monitoring for PR prolongation and CNS effects.
Contraindicated in severe renal impairment (GFR <30); use caution and monitor blood pressure when initiating in mild-to-moderate impairment
Adjusted according to creatinine clearance; supplemental dose after haemodialysis
Clearance reduced in moderate-to-severe renal impairment; use lower exposure and monitor for excess sedation and adverse effects
Monitor eGFR/serum creatinine and potassium; risk of acute kidney injury with volume depletion or renovascular disease
Baseline and periodic renal function; avoid in significant renal impairment and contraindicated in severe renal impairment due to nephrotoxicity risk
renal function before and during therapy; delayed clearance markedly increases toxicity — avoid in significant renal impairment
contraindicated in severe renal impairment (inadequate urinary drug delivery and hippurate/ammonia accumulation)

Predominantly renally cleared and accumulates in renal impairment; dosage reduction required with declining eGFR (moderate impairment ~<50, further with severe impairment ~<20); monitor renal function

Moxonidine and its metabolites are predominantly renally cleared and accumulate in renal impairment; use with caution and monitor blood pressure and renal function in moderate impairment, and avoid in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30).
In severe chronic renal impairment (GFR <25 mL/min) outside the immediate post-transplant period, avoid excessive MPA exposure; monitor full blood count and for MPA-related toxicity.
Use cautiously in renal impairment and titrate slowly; monitor blood pressure, heart rate and renal function.
Monitor renal function and serum potassium; perindoprilat clearance falls as eGFR declines, so cautious titration is required
Serum potassium and renal function; avoid or use with great caution in significant renal impairment due to reduced potassium excretion and hyperkalaemia risk
Renal function; pramipexole is predominantly renally cleared, so dosing frequency/amount must be reduced in impairment and it should be avoided in severe impairment without specialist guidance
Use a lower starting dose and monitor in significant renal impairment
Monitor serum phosphate and calcium; impaired renal excretion risks hyperphosphataemia, hypocalcaemia and metastatic calcification — avoid in severe renal impairment
Use with caution in renal impairment; monitor eGFR and serum potassium. Risk of acute kidney injury and hyperkalaemia, especially with volume depletion or renovascular disease.
If CrCl <30 mL/min, lower the initial exposure and titrate slowly. Monitor serum sodium.

Monitor renal function and urinalysis regularly; penicillamine is largely renally eliminated and nephrotoxic (membranous nephropathy/proteinuria) — avoid or use with great caution in significant renal impairment

Uricosuric efficacy depends on glomerular filtration; probenecid is largely ineffective and not recommended when eGFR is below ~30 mL/min/1.73m2. Ensure adequate hydration to reduce risk of uric acid crystallisation.
Renally excreted largely unchanged; requirements typically reduced in renal impairment and titrated to response, monitoring for cholinergic effects
Use lower starting dose and titrate cautiously in significant renal impairment
Not recommended when creatinine clearance/eGFR is below ~30-35 mL/min
Restricted exposure in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30); monitor for myopathy
Lower starting exposure and closer monitoring of renal function and potassium in severe renal impairment
Reduce dose and use with caution in moderate renal impairment; avoid in severe renal impairment
Use caution and avoid high doses in severe renal impairment due to increased myopathy risk
Use with caution in renal impairment; ensure adequate hydration to reduce crystalluria risk and monitor renal function and full blood count.
Intrinsically nephrotoxic; titrate to whole-blood trough levels and monitor eGFR/creatinine closely rather than by fixed renal threshold
Substantially renally eliminated; accumulation risk in significant renal impairment, use with caution and consider dose reduction
Monitor eGFR and potassium after initiation; risk of acute kidney injury with volume depletion, NSAIDs or renovascular disease
Dosing-interval adjustment required below eGFR 50; PrEP not recommended below CrCl 60 mL/min. Monitor serum creatinine, eGFR and serum phosphate
Dose-interval adjustment required below eGFR 50 mL/min; monitor renal function
Assess renal function before initiation and periodically; reduce dose in moderate-to-severe renal impairment
Use a lower starting dose and monitor renal function and serum potassium; risk of acute kidney injury with volume depletion or renal artery stenosis
Reduce exposure by interval extension and/or dose modification as renal function declines; guide by serum drug concentrations and renal function; accumulates in renal impairment
Dose reduction recommended when creatinine clearance is below 70 mL/min; supplemental dosing may be required on haemodialysis days as topiramate is dialysable
Reduce dose in moderate-to-severe renal impairment and in dialysis; monitor blood pressure and for accumulation of active metabolite
Renally eliminated; progressively reduce dose and monitor for CNS adverse effects (sedation, confusion) in mild-to-severe renal impairment
Reduce dose in moderate-to-severe renal impairment (eGFR <50); assess renal function before and periodically during therapy
Titrate slowly and use with caution in renal impairment; discontinue if acute renal failure or a clinically significant sustained rise in serum creatinine occurs
